label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So the question is about Jane's birthday and calculating a date 10 days before her 16th birthday. Let me break it down step by step. First, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, except in a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Leap years are divisible by 4, but 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, so no. So her birthday is February 28, 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So that would be 16 years after her birthdate. Adding 16 years to 2001 gives 2017. So her 16th birthday is February 28, 2017. The question is asking for the date 10 days before that. So if today is February 28, 2017, then 10 days prior would be... Let's count backwards. Starting from the 28th, subtract 10 days. Let me think: 28 minus 10 is 18. So that would be February 18, 2017. But wait, is there anything about leap years affecting February here? Well, 2017 isn't a leap year either, so February still has 28 days. So going back 10 days from February 28 would take us to February 18. Looking at the options, option B is 02/18/2017. But wait, let me double-check. Let me count the days step by step to make sure. Starting from February 28, going back 1 day is the 27th, then 26th, 25th, 24th, 23rd, 22nd, 21st, 20th, 19th, and then 18th on the 10th day. Wait, that's 10 days: from day 28, subtract 10 days would be 28 - 10 = 18. So yes, February 18. So option B is correct. But let me check the options again. The options are (A) 02/28/2017, which is her birthday, so 10 days before would not be that. (B) 02/18/2017, which matches my calculation. (C) is March, which doesn't make sense because going back 10 days from Feb 28 wouldn't go into March. Same with others like D, E, F. So B is the correct answer. Wait, but maybe I made a mistake in assuming that 2017 is not a leap year? Let me confirm: 2017 divided by 4 is 504.25. Not a whole number, so not a leap year.
